# VoidCrawl

> Rust-native CDP browser automation for Python via PyO3. Replaces Playwright and Selenium with a permissively-licensed (Apache-2.0) stack.

VoidCrawl is a Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) client written in Rust and exposed to Python via PyO3. Built on chromiumoxide with a shared Tokio runtime, it provides async-first browser automation with near-zero overhead tab reuse.

- Rust core, Python ergonomics (Python 3.10+)
- Tab pooling via BrowserPool for near-instant tab acquisition
- Stealth by default with clean Chrome flags
- No AGPL exposure -- MIT/Apache-2.0 licensed throughout
- Composable two-tier action framework (JS and CDP)

## Getting Started

- [Overview](/voidcrawl): Introduction, architecture, quick example, and FAQs
- [Installation](/voidcrawl/installation): Install from PyPI or build from source
- [Quick Start](/voidcrawl/quickstart): Automate a browser in minutes
- [Contributing](/voidcrawl/contributing): How to contribute to VoidCrawl

## Guides

- [Understanding the DOM](/voidcrawl/guides/understanding-the-dom): HTML vs. the DOM, why it matters for scraping, and how browser automation handles it
- [Browser Pool](/voidcrawl/guides/browser-pool): Cold start vs. tab reuse, pool configuration, and semaphore-bounded architecture
- [Async Native](/voidcrawl/guides/async-native): How Tokio and asyncio work together, common async patterns
- [Docker & VNC](/voidcrawl/guides/docker): Run Chrome headless or headful inside Docker with GPU passthrough and VNC viewing
- [Built-in Actions](/voidcrawl/guides/builtin-actions): DOM and CDP-level actions for clicking, typing, scrolling, and querying
- [Custom JS Actions](/voidcrawl/guides/custom-js-actions): Build reusable browser actions with JsActionNode, inline_js, and Flow composition
- [Stealth Mode](/voidcrawl/guides/stealth): Anti-detection patches and minimal-footprint automation
- [Cookbook](/voidcrawl/guides/cookbook): Task-oriented recipes for common VoidCrawl automation patterns

## Examples

- [Examples Overview](/voidcrawl/guides/examples): Runnable example scripts demonstrating common VoidCrawl patterns
- [Basic Navigation](/voidcrawl/guides/examples/basic-navigation): Launch a headless browser, visit a page, and read its content
- [DOM Interaction](/voidcrawl/guides/examples/dom-interaction): Query DOM elements, click buttons, and type into inputs
- [JavaScript Eval](/voidcrawl/guides/examples/javascript-eval): Evaluate arbitrary JavaScript in the page context
- [Screenshots](/voidcrawl/guides/examples/screenshots): Capture PNG screenshots of pages
- [Stealth Mode](/voidcrawl/guides/examples/stealth-mode): Compare browser fingerprints with stealth enabled vs. disabled
- [Actions Demo](/voidcrawl/guides/examples/actions-demo): Built-in actions, custom JS actions, Flows, and CDP-level actions
- [Docker Headful](/voidcrawl/guides/examples/docker-headful): Connect to Chrome running headful inside Docker with GPU and VNC
- [Multi-Page](/voidcrawl/guides/examples/multi-page): Work with multiple pages (tabs) in a single browser session

## API Reference

- [API Reference](/voidcrawl/reference/api-reference): Full API reference for VoidCrawl
- [Docker Config](/voidcrawl/reference/docker-config): Docker image, Compose, and VNC configuration reference

## Related Projects

- [Yosoi](/voidcrawl/yosoi): AI-powered CSS selector discovery that uses VoidCrawl for rendering JS-heavy pages
- [QScrape](/voidcrawl/qscrape): Fictional test websites for benchmarking scrapers

- [GitHub](https://github.com/CascadingLabs/VoidCrawl)
